content: Any updates on this? Our users have been facing this on Flutter version 3.27.1 as well.

> [@Priyantha-Kingslake](https://github.com/Priyantha-Kingslake) you are the very few who are able to reproduced the issue on a development device, Flutter team [@darshankawar](https://github.com/darshankawar) should work with you to get the reproduction details, e.g. Device exact details, Android version, whether the device is rooted etc, this will immensely help Flutter team to finally fix this never ending issue

I was working on VSCode and had no issues for running on debug mode, only got the issue installing via the apk. I got the stacktrace only once, and after that I couldn't get it again. However, I tried to run again on Android studio and then it showed the error. 

I solved the issue by following ChatGPT instructions and adding the following to `android/app/build.gradle`
```
defaultConfig {
    ...
    ndk {
        abiFilters 'armeabi-v7a', 'armeabi', 'arm64-v8a', 'x86_64'
    }
}
```

Note that I had the application running without issue on 4 different devices and only had the issue on a single device. After the fix the issue is now solved on that specific device, But I'm still uncertain that the real issue is solved.

Also found this article about a fix but I'm reluctant to do that as it seems to build the app as a 32bit even for 64bit capable devices. [Link](https://medium.com/codechai/flutter-app-couldnt-find-libflutter-so-c95ad81cbccd)

Would love to help more if I can.

> Samsung Galaxy A13
> Android 14
> Not rooted

Hi folks, at 300+ comments this thread has become a bit unmanageable for us. Due to the difficulty of distilling actionable feedback from this issue, and that the team has been making progress on individual instances as in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/168293 noted above, I'm going to close this issue. If you are still experiencing this problem, please file a new issue with full details.

In the age of Gemini, closing an issue on the grounds that 300+ comments are hard to distill feels unhelpful. Given that both Android and Flutter are maintained by Google, and that neither the suggested workarounds nor the fixes attempted by the Flutter team have addressed the problem, it’s concerning that a five year old issue remains unresolved but closed despite being among top 10 thumbs-upped issue

Closed? Really? It's just disgusting.

Who hired you, guys?

## iOS Dialog blur, brightness, and layout

Approximated standard iOS alert blur and brightness. Implemented correct overflow layout behavior for iOS alert content and buttons. Added blend mode to BoxDecoration. (#15431)

@xster @gspencergoog - Here's a PR to look at how the desired iOS alert layout can be implemented.  From here if there are ideas to achieve a significantly less complex layout, and/or a much more performant layout, please chime in.

There are still changes to be made: barrier color, 2 button stacking, enter animation, etc.  But the blur, brightness, and layout alone should be a good step in the right direction.
@xster I just pushed the render object implementation.  As mentioned, this is a rough and messy implementation - will clean and test pending your review of the high level structure.

@xster @Hixie - I cleaned up the render object implementation.

I still need to create the shared super class for LayoutId.
@matthew-carroll can we close this PR while you work on it? (I'm trying to clear out our review queue.)
@Hixie I suppose you can.  But isn't this what the queue is supposed to have? The PR isn't done in any sense, so closing it seems odd.

normally open PRs should be either waiting for review, waiting for bots, or waiting to land. If a PR has substantial work to be done it's generally better to close it, do the work, then open a new one when it's ready for review again. Otherwise the open "idle" PRs would drown out the ones waiting review (we have over 100 open PRs).
Ok, I'll close it for now.
@xster @gspencergoog @Hixie - After a series of refactors, test updates, and new test additions, I think this PR is once again ready for a legitimate review.
@Hixie @xster - Please let me know if there's anything else, or if we're good to merge.

@Hixie @xster I pushed another major set of changes to address the comments since the last push.  This push also includes some accessibility considerations regarding the dialog layout and button text wrapping in accessibility mode.

## Code Push / Hot Update / out of band updates

_This is currently not on Flutter's roadmap, for reasons discussed in these comments: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/14330#issuecomment-1279484739, https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/14330#issuecomment-485565194_

_Code push for Flutter is available as a third-party product from shorebird.dev, as discussed in this comment: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/14330#issuecomment-2099442823_

_This comment gives a brief overview of various kinds of "hot update" features, and gives terminology for referring to them, which can help if you wish to communicate unambiguously about this topic: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/14330#issuecomment-442274897_

----

Often people ask if Flutter supports "code push" or "hot update" or other similar names for pushing out-of-store updates to apps.

Currently we do not offer such a solution out of the box, but the primary blockers are not technological.  Flutter supports just in time (JIT) or interpreter based execution on both Android and iOS devices.  Currently we remove these libraries during --release builds, however we could easily include them.

The primary blockers to this feature resolve around current quirks of the iOS ecosystem which may require apps to use JavaScript for this kind of over-the-air-updates functionality.  Thankfully Dart supports compiling to JavaScript and so one could imagine several ways in which one compile parts of ones application to JavaScript instead of Dart and thus allows replacement of or augmentation with those parts in deployed binaries.

This bug tracks adding some supported solution like this.  I'll dupe all the other reports here.

We've built some basic prototypes here, but don't have anything really to share at this time.  To do this really well will require some work in Dart's compiler toolchain.  I would expect it to be many months before we will have much to share here.  The team is currently focused on stabilization for 1.0.

All that said, we hear you. :)  This is clearly a feature many people value and we're interested in providing functionality like this eventually.

How feasible would be to have a full .so replaced dynamically? The whole compiler toolchain, code shake etc shouldn't be steered to an arguably edge case that people may or may not benefit, given there will always be tickets at the top of the priority list.

I think there should be room for "sideloading" the full .so file, so the build from dart to machine code can still be exactly the same. A small but completely independent component could be assigned the  following "minimum viable features": download .so and replace the originally shipped one with it, and restart app with the main launch intent.

Extra responsibilities: download assets, verify signatures and what not. Update flutter/dart/skia engines and what not.

It sounds to me like it violates iOS guidelines, but I'm really not a fan of anything interpreted anyway, or iOS in general. And I'd rather have it in Android than not having it at all, just because it's cool.

At least the download part seems doable now that we have some isolate work being allowed in the background, but it would be interesting to see how to swap the original .so with the downloaded one. Maybe an extra .so or java code could do that part, but it's going to be sitting at the code folder, not sure the app itself can modify it so probably it would need some tweaks so the flutter engine loads all app's .so from internal data?
